title: C++ Foundations
description: Learn C++ from basic syntax through pointers, OOP, STL containers, and templates.

modules.title: Basics & Syntax
modules.description: Types, variables, control flow, and I/O.

sections.title: Data Types
sections.body_markdown: On most 64-bit systems, what is the typical output?
sections.explanation: `int` is typically 4 bytes and `double` is 8 bytes on most modern systems.

sections.title: References vs Values
sections.explanation: The `&` makes `x` a reference to `n`. Modifying `x` directly modifies `n`, so n becomes 6.

sections.title: For Loop
Switch Statement
sections.explanation: `x=2` matches `case 2`, prints 'B', then `break` exits the switch.

modules.title: Pointers & Memory
modules.description: Pointers, dynamic allocation, and memory management.

sections.title: Pointer Basics
sections.explanation: `&x` gets the address, stored in pointer `p`. `*p` dereferences the pointer, giving the value 42.

sections.title: new and delete
sections.explanation: `new int[3]` allocates a heap array. `arr[1]` accesses index 1 (value 20). `delete[]` frees the array.

sections.title: Smart Pointers
sections.body_markdown: Which smart pointer allows exactly ONE owner of a heap-allocated object?
sections.explanation: `unique_ptr` has exclusive ownership — it cannot be copied, only moved. It auto-deletes when it goes out of scope.

sections.title: Null Pointer
sections.explanation: `nullptr` evaluates to false in a boolean context, so the else branch prints 'null'.

modules.title: OOP in C++
modules.description: Classes, inheritance, polymorphism, and virtual functions.

sections.title: Class Basics
sections.explanation: The constructor initializer list sets `name` to 'Rex'. Public access allows direct printing.

sections.title: Virtual Functions
sections.explanation: `virtual` enables runtime polymorphism. The actual object is Child, so Child::speak() is called.

sections.title: Access Specifiers
sections.body_markdown: Which access specifier allows member access from derived classes but NOT from outside code?
sections.explanation: `protected` members are accessible within the class and its derived classes, but not from external code.

modules.title: STL & Templates
modules.description: Standard Template Library containers and basic templates.

sections.title: Vector Basics
sections.explanation: Vector starts with 3 elements, `push_back(4)` adds one more, giving size 4.

sections.title: Map Lookup
sections.explanation: `std::map` stores sorted key-value pairs. `m["a"]` retrieves the value 1.

sections.title: Function Templates
sections.explanation: The compiler generates two versions: `add<int>` returning 7 and `add<double>` returning 4.0.

sections.title: Range-Based For
